verified. All ~40 mentions of "Dutch IBAN" / "NL IBAN" / "NL-prefixed IBAN"

"European IBAN accepted by most Dutch employers and services".

in best-bank-account) is now fully resolved.

Claims about bunq (Dutch IBAN), N26 (German IBAN), and Revolut
(Lithuanian IBAN) are unchanged — these are factually correct.

commit.message:
fix: show meaningful messages in importer preview when no data or error (#139)

When GCP tiles return empty results (incompatible aggregation settings,
no recent metric data), the preview panel was stuck on "Loading preview…"
because _renderPreviewWidget bailed silently after calling _clearWidgetCanvas.
Same issue on API errors — only the status bar updated, not the preview area.

- Add _showPreviewMessage() helper to cleanly update the preview area
- Show GCP error detail when data.success is false
- Show "No data returned, try a longer window" when seriesCount is 0
- Only call _renderPreviewWidget when widgetData is actually present

Co-authored-by: tech <tech@tv.madhive.local>

fix: resolve auth loading hang and React Router redirect crash after Docker rebuilds

- Eagerly import auth pages (Login/Signup/Onboarding) — no lazy loading on critical path
- Use window.location.replace() for auth redirects instead of React Router <Navigate />,
  which silently crashed inside createBrowserRouter transitions
- GuestRoute renders immediately without waiting for auth loading state
- Add safety timeout (8s) and initCalled guard in AuthProvider
- Serve index.html with Cache-Control: no-cache to prevent stale HTML after rebuilds
- Add node_modules and .tsbuildinfo to .dockerignore to prevent Windows artifacts in Docker
- Add RootErrorBoundary to App for crash resilience

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore progress and error visibility via RunProcessWithStderrAsync

RestoreService was routing wimlib stdout through a pipe, causing wimlib
to suppress native \r-based progress (same root cause as the backup fix).
All stderr lines that weren't [WARNING] or GiB progress were silently
dropped, so registry restore errors went unseen.

Add RunProcessWithStderrAsync to IProcessRunner: stdout is not redirected
(wimlib inherits the real console handle and shows native progress),
stderr is piped and every line is delivered to the caller. RestoreService
now prints all stderr lines in red so file/registry errors are visible,
and shows a hint to run Extract if registry hives weren't restored.

commit.message:
fix: properly initialize lemon.js with client component + Url.Open

Root cause: createLemonSqueezy() wasn't being called after script load.
The previous anchor-click hack navigated away before lemon.js intercepted.

Fix:
- Add LemonSqueezyInit client component that calls createLemonSqueezy()
  on mount and after a 1s delay (covers script loading race)
- Mount it in root layout inside Providers
- Revert to Url.Open() which creates an iframe overlay (confirmed by
  reading lemon.js source - it ALWAYS creates iframe, never redirects)
- Wait for LemonSqueezy global before calling Url.Open()

commit.message:
Fix: embeddings np.float32→float, soporte XLSX/XLS/CSV, pymupdf fallback

- rag_chatbot.py: convierte np.float32 a float nativo en embed_documents()
  y embed_query() — corrige el error de ChromaDB al indexar

feat(i18n): expand to 8 languages with browser auto-detection

Italian (it), Simplified Chinese (zh), Japanese (ja) — ~1550 keys each.

Infrastructure changes:
- Locale store: type widened to 8 locales, SUPPORTED_LOCALES updated
- Translation hook: imports all 8 locale files
- Server i18n: supports all 8 locales via cookie
- Locale toggle: redesigned as dropdown with flag emojis and native names
  (replaces old en↔es toggle button)
- AI schemas: accept all 8 locales for AI response language
- AI route handlers: locale hint supports all 8 languages
- Application layer: locale types widened to string with "en" fallback
  for inline translations (cron, regex, git commit, etc.)

Auto-detection: navigator.language maps to closest supported locale
on first visit (e.g., "fr-FR" → "fr", "ja" → "ja").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: fall back to OpenF1 when FastF1 has no position data

FastF1 relies on Ergast API for race classification data, but Ergast
often lacks data for recent races. When FastF1 returns results with all
NaN positions (which happens when Ergast has no data), the ingestion
now falls back to OpenF1 which can derive positions from lap timing.

This fixes race results not being stored for the 2026 Australian GP
and similar recent races where Ergast data is delayed.

commit.message:
fix(bot): combine health and webhook on single port for Railway

Railway only exposes one PORT per service. Moved from separate health
(PORT) and webhook (PORT+1) servers to a single HTTP server that
handles both /health and /webhook on the same port.

Uses bot.createWebhook() callback instead of bot.launch({ webhook })
to allow sharing the HTTP server with the health endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix excess space: revert WeatherDetailsCards to natural height grid

Removing grid-rows-3 h-full [&>*]:h-full which created a circular
sizing dependency — the inner 1fr rows inflated WeatherDetailsCards
beyond WeeklyForecast's natural height, causing both columns to
stretch with wasted space. Back to plain grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 so
natural heights determine the row, with CSS Grid stretch keeping
both column bottoms aligned.

commit.message:
fix: type/gender search returning 0 results — three bugs

1. intent_parser_system_prompt.md: broaden recognized=true definition to
   include queries with type/gender/city/age/cost but no activity tags.
   'overnight girls camp' is a valid search, not unrecognized.

2. core/intent_parser.py (RC-1): only flip recognized=False when ALL tags
   were hallucinated AND no other structured params exist. Prevents
   type+gender searches from being silently discarded.

3. core/cssl.py: fix type filter — DB stores numeric codes ('1','2','3','4')
   not strings. Map: Day→'1'/'Day Camp', Overnight→'2'/'3', Both→'3'.
   Fix gender filter — most programs have gender=NULL (coed/unspecified);
   use soft match (gender=X OR NULL OR 0) instead of exact match.

commit.message:
refactor: comprehensive code quality pass — struct alignment, error handling, import ordering, idle detection, and redaction optimization

This commit encompasses a broad code quality improvement across 207 files
(2480 insertions, 1924 deletions) touching nearly every package in the
codebase.

Formatting and alignment:
- Align struct field tags and map literals to consistent column widths
  across config, ensemble, cost, serve, and robot packages
- Remove trailing blank lines and fix inconsistent whitespace
- Reorder imports to group standard library, third-party, and internal
  packages per Go conventions (serve/server.go, etc.)
- Remove unused trailing newlines in parser.go and other files

Error handling improvements:
- Handle previously-ignored return values from WebSocket conn.Close(),
  SetReadDeadline(), SetWriteDeadline(), and WriteMessage() in
  serve/server.go readPump/writePump with proper error logging
- Handle Close() errors in doctor API health check probe connections
- Add bounds check for agents array index in robot.go assignAgentsToPanes
  to prevent potential out-of-range panics

Feature additions:
- robot.go: add Python REPL prompt detection (">>>") to idle state
  determination via isPythonPrompt(), integrated into isIdlePrompt()
  and isPromptLine() for all agent types
- robot.go: refactor generateContextHints to use switch statement
  instead of if-else chain for cleaner control flow
- redaction.go: add literal prefilter optimization via
  passesLiteralPrefilter() to skip expensive regex evaluation when
  the input string doesn't contain required literal substrings
- cli/root.go: fix SendOptions.Redaction field path (was nested under
  SendOptions, now directly on opts)
- robot.go: fix CASS status timestamp access (drop .Time indirection)
- serve/server.go: close resource properly in performDoctorCheckAPI
- dashboard.go: simplify formatPaneLabel with direct TrimPrefix
- workflow/loader.go: add missing import

Test improvements:
- Update test expectations across e2e/, internal/cli/, internal/serve/,
  internal/ensemble/, internal/robot/ to match new struct layouts and
  error handling behavior
- Fix test helper alignment in multiple _test.go files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- nextjs.md: Next.js/Nuxt.js SSR security (Server Actions, middleware, env leaks)
- fastapi.md: FastAPI security (Depends auth, Pydantic gaps, CORS, async safety)
- frontend-security.md: SPA/client-side security (DOM XSS, postMessage, CSP, storage)

- cloud-native-security.md: Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, serverless, CI/CD
- supply-chain-advanced.md: Dependency confusion, typosquatting, lockfile integrity
- safe-patterns.md: Framework safe-by-default catalog for FP reduction
- data-flow-verification.md: 3-pass source→sink→sanitization protocol

New skill:
- rock-diff: Diff-based security review for PRs and CI/CD

Updated files:
- SKILL.md: Test dir exclusions, .agent-rock-ignore support, new framework loading
- stack-detection.md: Next.js, FastAPI, Flask, NestJS, AI/ML, IaC, Frontend SPA detection
- README.md: Fixed installation (includes rock-quick/rock-deep/rock-diff), new features

Added R&D documentation:
